notes: Authentication used the supplied admin credentials and necessarily created/touched normal auth session/login/audit metadata.
Post-login certification avoided save/submit/delete/approve/reject/reset/activate/deactivate/promote/run/migration/toggle actions.
Sidebar favorites are localStorage-only; the final sweep used a fresh browser context and did not click sidebar favorite controls.

Creditor Obligations Manage regulatory requirements for creditors and data providers Add Creditor Obligation All Jurisdictions Federal Nova Scotia Ontario All Duty Types ACCURACY CORRECTION INVESTIGATION NOTIFICATION NOTIFICATION Nova Scotia - CRA s. 13 Upon completing the investigation of a disputed item, the furnisher must notify the consumer of the outcome. The notification must detail: (1) the results of the investigation; (2) any changes made to the consumer's information; (3) the consumer's right to request that corrected information be sent to those who previously received reports. The notification must be provided upon

Bureau Obligations Manage regulatory requirements for credit reporting agencies Add Bureau Obligation DESCRIPTION TYPE ACTIONS In Northwest Territories, credit bureaus operate under federal PIPEDA and voluntarily apply standards from provincial Consumer Reporting Acts. Consumers have rights to access their credit files, dispute inaccurate information, and have information corrected or removed when appropriate. No specific territorial credit reporting legislation; federal law applies GENERAL In Nunavut, credit bureaus operate under federal PIPEDA and voluntarily apply standards from provincial Consumer Reporting Acts. Consumers

Debt Collection Agency Obligations Manage regulatory requirements for debt collection agencies Add Debt Collection Agency Obligation DESCRIPTION TYPE ACTIONS Debt collectors must cease all collection communications within 5 days of receiving a written cease communication request from the consumer. Upon receipt of such request, collectors must: (1) immediately stop all telephone calls, letters, and other communications; (2) document the request and date of receipt; (3) update account systems to prevent automated communications; (4) train staff on the cease request; (5) only communicate to confirm cessation or notify of specific
